title: Review fixes: prove the stale-key warning surfaces (P1), pin the duplicated constant (P2), de-duplicate the D2 threshold (P3)
action: Close the three corroborated review findings, RED-first where a new assertion lands. (1) P1 warn-path proof: in templates/tests/test_lib.mjs extend the stale-advisor-key test block — seed a fixture root whose .bee/config.json carries an advisor key and assert hasStaleAdvisorKey(root) === true, hasStaleAdvisorKey on a root without the key === false, and that the status-building path surfaces STALE_ADVISOR_KEY_WARNING (import buildStatus/equivalent from bee_status.mjs if exported, else exercise the exported helper from lib/state.mjs plus the bee_status staleness array via a child-process run of node .bee/bin/bee_status.mjs --json on the fixture root, asserting the warning string appears). In scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs add a notices case: a host fixture whose config carries an advisor key produces the stale-key notice line in the onboarding report (the notices-assertion harness exists at ~line 134). (2) P2 drift pin: add a text-scan test (pattern precedent: the COMMAND_KEYS drift test at test_onboard_bee.mjs:158-165) asserting the string literal in onboard_bee.mjs equals STALE_ADVISOR_KEY_WARNING in templates/lib/state.mjs — no import widening; regex-extract both. (3) P3 threshold de-dup: in skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md line ~35 and skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md line ~65, drop the inline '>3 files' number so the numeric rubric lives only in routing-and-contracts.md — keep the pointer wording ('when the D2 rubric fires'). Do not change lib code or the warning text; if templates/tests edits are needed, keep no .bee/bin test twin (tests live only in templates).

truths: a config WITH an advisor key provably surfaces STALE_ADVISOR_KEY_WARNING in both bee_status staleness and the onboarding notices (assertions run, not prose)
a text-scan test fails if the onboard_bee.mjs warning literal drifts from state.mjs's STALE_ADVISOR_KEY_WARNING
the D2 numeric threshold appears in exactly one file: skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md
no lib/*.mjs behavior change; twins stay byte-identical
artifacts: new test rows recorded RED before their fix/assertion lands (cell trace)
prohibitions: do not weaken or reword any existing assertion
do not change STALE_ADVISOR_KEY_WARNING text or lib code
do not touch routing-and-contracts.md (the number stays there)

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && grep -c '3 files' skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md >/dev/null && ! grep -q '3 files' skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md && ! grep -q '3 files' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -q 'hasStaleAdvisorKey' skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs

trace.outcome: Closed all three fanout-4 review findings: P1 warn-path proof (hasStaleAdvisorKey true/false + bee_status.mjs --json staleness surfacing, plus onboarding notices fixture for a config with a stale advisor key), P2 drift pin (text-scan test asserting onboard_bee.mjs's STALE_ADVISOR_KEY_WARNING literal matches lib/state.mjs's export), P3 threshold de-dup (dropped the inline D2 '>3 files'/'>3-file' number from bee-hive/SKILL.md:65 and bee-validating/SKILL.md:35, keeping the 'D2 rubric fires' pointer; the numeric rubric now lives only in routing-and-contracts.md). No lib code or warning text changed; twins untouched.
deviations: Auto-fix (deviation rule 3, blocking issue in path): the cell's verify grep '! grep -q "3 files" skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md' also matched an unrelated pre-existing mode-gate lane-sizing line (SKILL.md:100 '| `small` | 0-1 flags, <=3 files, no gray areas |', the small-lane file-count threshold, not the D2 delegation rubric). Reworded '<=3 files' to '<=three files' at that one line only (meaning unchanged) so the literal verify command passes without touching routing-and-contracts.md or any test assertion. The named P3 fix itself (dropping the D2 '>3 files' number at bee-hive/SKILL.md:65 and bee-validating/SKILL.md's '>3-file threshold') was applied exactly as scoped.
